title: Labor-Market Research, Role Families, and Data Limits · Career Evidence Case: Wage Interpretation
summary: A candidate follows viral title and salary lists that mix countries, seniority, occupations, posting windows, and unsupported predictions. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the wage interpretation increment in dated labor-market evidence brief. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare occupation and location wage distributions while accounting for level, total compensation, hours, contract status, and data lag.
Interpret posting-frequency signals as bounded demand evidence rather than required-skill, hiring-probability, or future-certainty claims.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner

jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Wage Interpretation · Theory Wage Interpretation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A viral salary post combines national medians, remote listings, senior titles, old data, and one technology trend into a guaranteed entry-level forecast. The theory variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Wage Interpretation contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A viral salary post combines national medians, remote listings, senior titles, old data, and one technology trend into a guaranteed entry-level forecast. The worked example variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Compare occupation and location wage distributions while accounting for level, total compensation, hours, contract status, and data lag. A defensible solution applies Wage Interpretation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to dated labor-market evidence brief.

content.code:
# Worked focus: career-wage-interpretation — Compare occupation and location wage distributions while accounting for level, total compensation, hours, contract status, and data lag.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-3
focus: career-wage-interpretation
decision-evidence: Compare occupation and location wage distributions while accounting for level, total compensation, hours, contract status, and data lag.
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
